titleStmt.title: SII 9.439: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 439. (A.R. No. 804 of 1917.) ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD TO THE EAST OF THE VILLAGE GAUNIVARIPALLI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is damaged and dated Śaka 100 30 39 (i.e., 1339.) Hēmaḷambi, Phālguṇa, ba. 10, G[uruvāra] corresponding to A.D. 1418 March 2 Wednesday (not Thursday), in the reign of Dēvarāya-Mahārāya, son of Harihara-Mahārāya, who was the son of Bukkarāya. It records that the king confirmed the exemption granted by Harihara-Mahārāya, as a reward for having manufactured the throne in the royal court (hajāra-siṁhāsana), to the Pāṁchālas from taxes on bullocks, buffaloes, carts, jewels, etc.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
subhaśakavaruṣa
100 3039 Āgunēṭi
hēmalaṁbisaṁvacara pālguṇa
ba 10 gu svasti śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājapa
ramēsvara śrīvīrapratāpa bukharāyara komāra A
riAramahārāyara garbhapunyōdaya dēvarā
yamahārāyaru yappattunālku Āhanada paṁ
cālakhe koṭa sāsanakramaventendare muṁ
na Ariharamahārāyaru bhikṣāvritti tā
tayya Āru dariśana Ādineṁṭa samaya
muṁtāgi pāṁcālake hajārasihmāsa
nava māḍidalli ma(ṁ)nnisidaṁtā mane
kudure yettu yeṁme tottu baṁḍi
Ābaraṇa muṁtāda
da paṁcalōhakhe IṁtāThe slab is broken after this.
Digital edition of SII 9.439 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
